title: The Meridian Door
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A cartographer steps through a door in a London basement and into a world where geography is written by belief.
long_description: Dr. Helena Rice is a cartographer who believes in precision: every line on a map should mean something. When she discovers a door in the basement of the Royal Geographical Society that opens onto a world where mountains move and rivers appear where people believe they should be, her certainties unravel. In the land of Meridia, the land itself answers to story and faith, and a warlord is using an ancient map to redraw reality. Helena must learn to map a world that refuses to stay still, and in doing so, discover that the places we carry inside us are as real as any charted territory. A lyrical portal fantasy about the stories that shape the world.
